name: Transmit Power Control
description: Sub-sector template for transmit power control patents including closed-loop TPC, open-loop power control, link adaptation, interference-aware power management, and power headroom reporting.
contextDescription: Transmit power control is fundamental to cellular networks — every UE and base station adjusts transmission power continuously. In LTE/5G NR, open-loop power control sets initial power based on path loss estimation, while closed-loop TPC commands from the gNB fine-tune UE transmit power. 3GPP TS 38.213 defines UL power control for NR; TS 36.213 for LTE. Power headroom reports (PHR) inform the network of UE power capability. In dual connectivity (EN-DC, NR-DC), power allocation across multiple connections is complex and patent-rich. Key targets: every modem vendor, every base station vendor.
scoringGuidance: TPC is mandatory for every UE — universal infringement surface across all cellular devices.
Dual connectivity power allocation patents are high-value: EN-DC is the dominant 5G deployment mode.
Interference-aware power control (eICIC, ICIC) patents target HetNet deployments — broad applicability.
ML-based power optimization patents are forward-looking but increasingly relevant for O-RAN.
Power headroom reporting is exercised continuously — PHR patents have strong detectability.

questions.question: What level of transmit power control innovation does this patent provide? Score 8-10 for novel closed-loop/open-loop hybrid algorithms, fractional power control, or multi-parameter TPC. 5-7 for improvements to standard TPC command processing or accumulation. 3-4 for basic open-loop or closed-loop power control. 1-2 for general power adjustment without TPC specificity.
questions.reasoningPrompt: TPC commands (in DCI) adjust UE power every subframe. Novel accumulation logic or hybrid approaches are high-value.

questions.question: Does this patent address uplink power control, downlink power control, or a unified framework? Score 8-10 for unified UL/DL power management framework or joint optimization. 5-7 for uplink-specific power control (more complex, more patent activity). 3-4 for downlink power allocation. 1-2 for unspecified or general power concepts.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Uplink power control is more complex and patent-dense than downlink. Unified frameworks are rare and valuable.

questions.question: Does this patent address power allocation across dual connectivity (EN-DC, NR-DC) or carrier aggregation? Score 8-10 for dynamic power splitting across MCG/SCG or cross-carrier power management. 5-7 for CA-aware power control or per-component-carrier power. 3-4 for single connection power with awareness of multi-connectivity. 1-2 for single connection only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: EN-DC power sharing (PHR Type 3) is exercised by every NSA 5G device — very high infringement surface.

questions.question: Does power control integrate interference management (ICIC, eICIC, CoMP)? Score 8-10 for novel interference-aware power allocation or coordinated power control across cells. 5-7 for power control considering neighbor cell interference. 3-4 for interference mentioned as motivation. 1-2 for no interference management aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Interference management through power control is essential for HetNet and dense deployments.

questions.question: Does this patent use ML/AI-based power optimization versus traditional control theory? Score 8-10 for reinforcement learning or neural network-based power control. 5-7 for data-driven or adaptive algorithms beyond traditional control. 3-4 for statistical methods. 1-2 for purely analytical/formula-based approach.
questions.reasoningPrompt: ML-based power control is emerging in O-RAN and Rel-18 — forward-looking but increasingly relevant.

questions.question: How closely does this patent align with 3GPP TPC command framework (TS 38.213, TS 36.213)? Score 8-10 for direct implementation of standardized power control procedures or PHR. 5-7 for enabling standard compliance. 3-4 for standard-adjacent power concepts. 1-2 for proprietary power management approach.
questions.reasoningPrompt: TPC is heavily standardized in 3GPP — identify specific clauses and procedures.

questions.question: Is the power control behavior observable through protocol analysis? Score 8-10 for behavior visible in power headroom reports, TPC commands in DCI, or SRS measurements. 5-7 for behavior inferable from UE power profiles or conformance testing. 3-4 for internal baseband power algorithms. 1-2 for algorithm-level logic only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: TPC commands are in every DCI and PHR is mandatory reporting — highly observable in protocol captures.
